findings.summary: Pill retone collapses TrackedSubmissions's 6-tone status ladder to 4 visible tones — submitted, approved, paid all render identically (fuchsia-deep), breaking the lifecycle disambiguation the comment claims is locked.
findings.evidence: STATUS_TONE maps draft→neutral, posted→info, submitted→fuchsia, approved→success, rejected→danger, paid→success. After v0.7.32 Pill retone: success=fuchsia (same Tailwind classes as the 'fuchsia' tone — bg-fuchsia-soft text-fuchsia-deep border-fuchsia/30). So 'submitted', 'approved', and 'paid' all paint the same chip. The header comment at line 25 still asserts '6-tone status ladder (locked 2026-05-29): paid+approved=success green, submitted=fuchsia'. The comment is now a lie, and clipper UX walking the table cannot distinguish 'in flight with Whop' from 'approved-but-unpaid' from 'paid' without reading the literal status text.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) collapse the STATUS_TONE comment + map to the new 3-visible-tone reality and add a leading lucide icon (Clock for submitted, Check for approved, DollarSign for paid) so the icon does the work the colour used to, OR (b) introduce a brand-pure 'positive' tone that visually differs from base fuchsia (e.g. fuchsia-soft FILL with white CHECK icon at higher contrast vs. fuchsia-outline pending) and re-map approved/paid to it. Either way, the comment block at lines 25-31 must stop claiming a 6-tone ladder that no longer renders.

findings.summary: Silent at-rest state for `source_exists === false` when the project is `done` — the user loses the v0.7.8 'Source missing' eyebrow with no compensating signal anywhere on the resting card.
findings.evidence: The retoned chip rules render ONLY: (a) `pipeline_failed` → danger chip, (b) `!project.done` → dim 'In progress' chip, (c) otherwise → no TL chip. There is no branch for `done && source_exists === false`. A completed project whose source file the user later moved/trashed now renders identical to a healthy ready project — no chip, no hover-overlay flag (the overlay shows filename + clips_count + editedAt only). Per the previous gate comment at the old line 138: 'clips on disk still play, but a re-run won't work' — Daniel ENABLES the re-run from this state and the failure is silent until the run actually fires. The brief says 'Source missing folded into the dim In progress semantics', but that fold is incorrect: a `done` project with missing source never enters the `!done` branch.
findings.reproduce: cd ~/Desktop/jnr/desktop && grep -n 'source_exists\|pipeline_failed\|project.done' src/components/cockpit/LibraryCard.tsx — observe the absence of any source_exists branch in the new chip rules.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) restore the muted source-missing chip as a SECOND TL row gated on `project.source_exists === false`, OR (b) surface 'source missing' in the hover-overlay subtitle so the calm wall stays silent but the hover discloses it. Option (b) keeps the calm-wall mandate intact. The current state is a silent-success regression — the user only finds out the source moved AFTER they try to re-run.

findings.summary: Brief claims 'imported' provenance is 'surfaced in the hover meta line' — but the hover overlay code never references project.imported. Imported packs are now indistinguishable from cut-from-source packs at ANY zoom level.
findings.evidence: Hover overlay renders `{project.source_filename}` + `{project.clips_count} clip(s) · {editedAt}` only. No 'Imported' eyebrow, no Layers icon, nothing. The brief's mitigation ('surfaced in the hover meta line') was not implemented. Stale top-of-file comments at lines 1-2 still describe the old 'Imported · N clips' chip + pip behaviour, which is misleading future readers.
findings.reproduce: cd ~/Desktop/jnr/desktop && grep -n 'imported\|Imported' src/components/cockpit/LibraryCard.tsx — only the stale header comments and the chip-rule rationale reference imported; nothing reaches the DOM.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a single 'Imported' eyebrow word to the hover overlay subtitle when `project.imported`, e.g. `{project.imported && 'Imported · '}{project.clips_count} clip(s) · {editedAt}`. Also delete or rewrite the lines 1-2 file-header comments to reflect the v0.7.32 reality.

findings.summary: Stale comment claims a 'faint diagonal stripe pattern (18s linear) for living world depth' — the stripes were deleted in this same diff. Future readers will look for a missing layer.
findings.evidence: AuroraBackground.tsx:1-15 header comment still describes 'two layered radial gradient blobs drift in counter-direction (14s alternate) under a faint diagonal stripe pattern (18s linear)'. The render at line 17-23 only has lc-aurora-blobs. Brief acknowledged: 'Comments still mention the stripes — that's stale but not functional.' Confirmed.
findings.suggested_fix: Trim the comment to: 'Two layered radial gradient blobs drift in counter-direction (14s alternate) — living-world depth without paid runtime. The diagonal-stripe overlay was removed in v0.7.32 as impeccable bans repeating-linear-gradient decoration.' Two-line surgical edit.

findings.summary: Pattern-grep follow-up: the diff fixes 4 surfaces' yellow/green/blue palette, but the SAME #F59E0B amber pattern survives in 9+ other active surfaces. Calling this an 'impeccable audit pass' while these stand is half-done.
findings.evidence: grep -rEn '#(EAB308|FACC15|10B981|34D399|F59E0B|f59e0b|3B82F6|60A5FA)' desktop/src --include='*.tsx' --include='*.css' (excl. _backups) — 30+ matches in: src/components/schedule/ChannelPicker.tsx:186-198+265-272, src/components/schedule/ChannelRow.tsx:78+85, src/components/schedule/AddChannelModal.tsx:356, src/components/clips-feed/GridMasterToolbar.tsx:706+754, src/components/clips-feed/InlineScheduler.tsx:650+668, src/components/cockpit/AvatarOrbit.tsx:79, src/components/cockpit/ActivityOrbitParticles.tsx:12, src/components/cockpit/BottomCockpit.tsx:240, src/components/upload/DirectPublishQueue.tsx:416-417, src/components/upload/LocalQueue.tsx:251+500+589+631-632, src/components/workbench/AccountBindingChip.tsx:552+568, src/components/upload/ClipReadyCard.tsx:261, src/index.css:255+298-300 (earn deck amber tail). The brand-kit skill DOES sanction #F59E0B for warn states ("Amber #f59e0b is allowed for warn/pending"), so the diff's choice to retone Pill warning → fuchsia is actually TIGHTER than brand-kit policy — which is fine ONLY if it's done consistently. Today: Pill.tsx says warn=fuchsia, but 9+ schedule/cockpit/upload surfaces still paint warn=amber. The brand voice is now self-contradicting.
findings.suggested_fix: Pick a policy and apply it everywhere in the same ship: EITHER (A) follow brand-kit literally — amber stays for warn/pending states, and Pill.tsx warning tone goes back to amber (#F59E0B), OR (B) follow the diff's stricter line — every #F59E0B in active src migrates to var(--color-warn) / var(--color-warn-soft) tokens with fuchsia-deep semantics. Option B is consistent with the diff's stated 'fuchsia-only restraint pattern'; option A is what the brand-kit skill currently says. Either way, the schedule/cockpit/upload surfaces must move in lockstep with Pill — otherwise the warn palette splits across the app.

findings.summary: Internal StatusChip danger variant uses hardcoded #DC2626 hex — same color the diff just promoted to a CSS token. Consistency miss.
findings.evidence: Line 225: `bg-[#DC2626]/85 text-white shadow-[0_0_18px_rgba(220,38,38,0.5)]` — hardcoded. The diff added `--color-danger: #dc2626` precisely to centralise this hue (per Pill.tsx and bounty-fit.tsx). LibraryCard's StatusChip was not touched in the same pass. Same hardcoded #DC2626 also at LibraryCard.tsx:275 (RingButton destructive border/hover), FirstRun.tsx:230 (`text-[#DC2626]` for activation error), and ClipPreview-style error paths elsewhere.
findings.suggested_fix: Migrate the 4-5 hardcoded #DC2626 sites in active src to `var(--color-danger)` / `var(--color-danger-bright)` in the same commit. Otherwise the token addition is half-applied and a future colour change requires hunting all the inline hexes again.

findings.summary: TR chip rule drops the previously-persistent 'Archived' chip and relies on `opacity-70` alone to signal archived state — but the archived card's wall reading is now `opacity-70 hover:opacity-100`, meaning archived cards look identical to ready cards on hover.
findings.evidence: Line 84: `${project.archived ? 'opacity-70 hover:opacity-100' : ''}`. On hover the archived card pops to opacity-100, indistinguishable from a normal card. Comment at line 144 claims 'card opacity already signals archived' — true at rest, false on hover. A user pointer-scanning the wall WILL hover-disambiguate, and the archived-ness vanishes precisely when they're paying most attention.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) drop the hover:opacity-100 modifier so archived stays muted at all states, OR (b) add a small 'archived' eyebrow word into the hover overlay subtitle (similar to the P2-003 imported fix) so the hover-disambiguation surfaces the state instead of erasing it. Option (a) is the minimal change.

_legacy_v0_7_14_block_below_kept_for_aud.title: PlatformBadge orphan — no parent surface mounts it
_legacy_v0_7_14_block_below_kept_for_aud.resolution: RESOLVED. ClipCard.tsx line 441 mounts PlatformBadge at absolute bottom-left gated on `clip.platforms && clip.platforms.length > 0`. ClipWindow.tsx line 184 mounts PlatformBadge at absolute top-right (z-10, pointer-events-none) on the same gate. Both gates correctly hide the badge in the default empty-array / undefined state.

_legacy_v0_7_14_block_below_kept_for_aud.title: PlatformBadge has no data producer — types without Python writers
_legacy_v0_7_14_block_below_kept_for_aud.resolution: RESOLVED. sidecar.py adds method_set_clip_platforms (validates the 6 PlatformIds — youtube/tiktok/instagram/x/linkedin/facebook — with no escape hatch; rejects unknown ids via ValueError). Registered in METHODS dict at sidecar.py:2795. project.py create_imported_pack now writes `platforms: []` + `overlay_template: None` defaults on every imported clip (project.py:527-528). sidecar.ts adds applyOverlayTemplate + setClipPlatforms wrappers with signatures matching the Python params exactly.

_legacy_v0_7_14_block_below_kept_for_aud.title: OverlayTemplateGallery / OverlayTemplateKey union has no Python handler
_legacy_v0_7_14_block_below_kept_for_aud.resolution: RESOLVED at the data layer. sidecar.py adds method_apply_overlay_template (sidecar.py:1227-1273) with the canonical _OVERLAY_TEMPLATE_PRESETS dict mapping all 8 OverlayTemplateKey values to layout+position. None-template clear path handled (sidecar.py:1255-1258 — persists overlay_template = None and returns project). Unknown templates raise ValueError. The real-bake path routes through stages.apply_overlay_to_clip (verified to exist at stages.py:2015) when source_path is provided. Mount of OverlayTemplateGallery itself is explicitly deferred to v0.7.15 per Claude's note — data layer ready, UI mount pending. Acceptable per the defer-list pre-approval.

_legacy_v0_7_14_block_below_kept_for_aud.title: CANVAS_GRID_ROWS too small for Kimi's 20-tile cap
_legacy_v0_7_14_block_below_kept_for_aud.resolution: RESOLVED. workbench/types.ts raises CANVAS_GRID_ROWS 8 → 10. Comment notes 8 cols × 10 rows = 80 cells, fitting 20 × 2×2 tiles cleanly. Verified findFreeSlot loop bounds (useWorkbenchStore.ts:84-85) use the symbol, not a magic number — auto-adjusts. clampPos (useWorkbenchStore.ts:64-65) uses CANVAS_GRID_ROWS - size.h — correctly enforces new bound. WindowManager.tsx:336 sets gridTemplateRows: repeat(CANVAS_GRID_ROWS, ...) — visual grid matches logical bound. Persisted session restore path clamps incoming pos through clampPos so older 8-row layouts stay valid; no migration needed.

_legacy_v0_7_14_block_below_kept_for_aud.title: PlatformBadgePicker has no real data path — Kimi's component was decorative
_legacy_v0_7_14_block_below_kept_for_aud.resolution: RESOLVED. ClipPreview.tsx:987 mounts PlatformBadgePicker. onToggle (lines 989-998) computes the next array (toggle semantics), awaits sidecar.setClipPlatforms(slug, index, next), threads the new project via onProjectChange(r.project), and catches errors via setActionError(humanError(e)) — uses the canonical humanError pipeline, no raw String(e). The picker is reachable both via ResultsGrid (clip click → ClipPreview modal) and ClipEditDrawer (workbench tile → edit → ClipPreview).

new_findings.title: Silent strand — cut-from-source clips never get platforms / overlay_template defaults written
new_findings.severity: P2 (soft strand, not a hard bug — UI gates defend correctly)
new_findings.claim_implicit: Defaults persist on every clip via the imported-pack pathway.
new_findings.actual: llm.py:503-513 builds the cut-from-source clip dict (out_clips.append({...})) with NO platforms / overlay_template fields. The dict is persisted via project.set_clips() unchanged. So `clip.platforms` is undefined (not empty array) for cut-from-source clips. The TS type marks both optional, so this does not crash, AND the ClipCard + ClipWindow gates use `clip.platforms && clip.platforms.length > 0` which correctly evaluates falsy for undefined. PlatformBadgePicker uses `clip.platforms ?? []` so the picker still works. Net effect: end-user can still pick platforms on cut-from-source clips via the picker, but the underlying record shape is inconsistent between import and cut paths.
new_findings.evidence: llm.py:503-513 (cut-from-source clip factory), project.py:520-528 (import-pack defaults — note import is the ONLY path that writes the defaults), ClipCard.tsx:441 + ClipWindow.tsx:184 (truthy-array gates), ClipPreview.tsx:988 (?? [] coalescing)
new_findings.fix_required: Add `"platforms": []` + `"overlay_template": None` to the out_clips.append() dict in llm.py:503 — same 2-line surgical fix that landed on project.py:527-528. Keeps record shape consistent across both factory paths. No UI changes needed.
new_findings.defer_rationale: UI defends correctly via ?? [] coalescing and truthy-length gates — soft-strand only. Promote to P0 the moment any downstream code (publish routing, analytics aggregation, drip planner) starts iterating clip.platforms WITHOUT the nullish-coalescing guard.

new_findings.title: PlatformBadge TikTok glyph still renders Latin 'T' — pre-existing design regression carried into v0.7.14
new_findings.severity: P2 (cosmetic, noted in prior cycle as P2)
new_findings.claim_implicit: PlatformBadge ships with brand-correct icons for all 6 platforms.
new_findings.actual: PlatformBadge.tsx:11 + 48-49 + 102-103: TikTok has no Lucide icon, so the badge renders a literal Latin 'T' in a teal circle. Once founders see this they will read it as a placeholder glyph rather than the TikTok wordmark or musical-note. Carried over from v0.7.13 untouched.
new_findings.fix_required: Swap the 'T' fallback for an inline SVG of TikTok's monogram (the music-note glyph is 6 lines of SVG). Picker side has the same issue at line 103.
new_findings.defer_rationale: Cosmetic. Brand polish, not data correctness.

verdict_rationale: v0.7.32 impeccable audit pass — 2 P1 findings block ship: (P1-001) TrackedSubmissions's 6-tone status ladder is collapsed to 4 visible tones by the Pill retone with no compensating icon — submitted/approved/paid all paint identical fuchsia chips, breaking lifecycle disambiguation. (P1-002) `source_exists === false` on a `done` project is now silent at rest — the v0.7.8 'Source missing' eyebrow was dropped and the brief's claimed 'folded into In progress' is incorrect because the `!done` branch never fires for completed-but-source-moved projects. (P1-005) Pattern-grep shows 9+ schedule/cockpit/upload surfaces still paint #F59E0B amber for warn states — calling this 'impeccable' while half the warn palette stays amber and the other half is fuchsia means the brand voice contradicts itself. The 4 P2 findings (stale aurora comments, missing imported eyebrow, archived-on-hover indistinguishable, #DC2626 not migrated to token) are soft but should land in the same commit since they're identical-cost surgical edits. No iron-gate sentinels removed. No type errors. Vite build clean.
v0_7_15_punch_list: P2-1 — Add `platforms: []` + `overlay_template: None` to llm.py:503 out_clips.append() so cut-from-source path matches import-pack record shape
P2-2 — Replace 'T' Latin letter fallback for TikTok in PlatformBadge.tsx:49 + :103 with inline SVG monogram
K4 — Mount LibraryQuickPreview into LibraryTab modal-on-tile-click
K5 — Native port BountySwipe into Earn tab (or accept webview Earn stays canonical)
K6 — Wire StudioTour with real [data-tour] anchors, replace OnboardingOverlay
K7 — Mount ActivityOrbit into WorkstationRoom avatar slot
OverlayTemplateGallery — UI mount + wire onSelect → sidecar.applyOverlayTemplate (data layer already ready)
F5 — sidecar auto-restart (60-90min Rust + TS, 1-retry cap)
ship_recommendation_legacy_v0_7_14: Ready to commit v0.7.14 and push pending Daniel's explicit approval per the No-push-until-confirmed rule. The Daniel-review build at /Applications/Liquid Clips.app is already at 0.7.14 ready for the live walk.
ship_recommendation_v0_7_32: Resolve P1-001 (Pill tone collapse breaking TrackedSubmissions ladder), P1-002 (silent source-missing on done projects), and P1-005 (warn-palette schism between Pill and 9+ schedule/cockpit/upload surfaces) BEFORE shipping. P2-003 + P2-004 + P2-006 + P2-007 are identical-cost surgical edits that should ride the same commit. None of the findings touch iron-gate sentinels or break the type-check / vite build.
